purpose: Standalone growth-stock board for emerging public companies with large upside potential. Expanded mandate: full market scan, daily additions, 40-50 name target.

macroRules.description: Graham's macro overlay — applied at every 5-min cron check-in. Factor these conditions into all trading decisions.

rules.action: Increase caution. No new full-size entries. Prefer watching over acting. Tighten any loose stops.
Reduce risk. No adds. Trim any extended positions. Hold cash. Fear is elevated — wait for stabilization.
Defensive posture. Only hold highest-conviction names. Cash is a position.

rules.condition: 10Y yield rising sharply (>10bps in a day)
rules.action: Growth stocks reprice lower. Reduce high-multiple names (PLTR, AXON). Prefer value / cash-flow names.
EM and LatAm exposure (MELI, NU) under pressure from currency headwinds. Reduce size.
Credit stress signal — divergence is a warning. Reduce risk, especially in speculative names.

rules.condition: TLT rallying while SPY selling off
rules.action: Flight to safety. Defensive mode. Bonds confirming equity fear — stay cautious.

markets.name: S&P 500 ETF (SPDR)
markets.whyItMatters: The benchmark for U.S. equities. Sets the tone for every sector, every day. When SPY moves, everything moves. It's the tide.
markets.signalWatched: Trend direction (above/below 50-day and 200-day MA). Key level: 200-day MA. Down 1%+ on the day triggers caution mode. Down 2%+ triggers defensive posture.
markets.cronRule: Check at every 5-min interval. If SPY -1%+ intraday: increase caution. If SPY -2%+: defensive.

markets.name: Nasdaq 100 ETF (Invesco)
markets.whyItMatters: Represents the top 100 non-financial Nasdaq stocks — the heart of tech and growth investing. If QQQ is weak, growth stocks are weak.
markets.signalWatched: QQQ vs SPY relative strength. When QQQ underperforms, risk appetite is fading. Key: 50-day MA as support/resistance.
markets.cronRule: Track vs SPY spread. Underperformance of QQQ signals tech rotation or growth-to-value shift.

markets.name: Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF (SPDR)
markets.whyItMatters: 30 blue-chip industrials. DIA strength without QQQ = rotation to value. Useful for spotting market leadership shifts.
markets.signalWatched: DIA vs QQQ spread. When DIA leads, rotation is value/defensive. When QQQ leads, growth is in favor.

markets.name: Russell 2000 ETF (iShares)
markets.whyItMatters: Small-cap gauge. IWM is the 'risk appetite' canary. When small-caps lead, it signals confidence and economic expansion. When they lag, risk appetite is shrinking.
markets.signalWatched: IWM vs SPY relative strength. IWM leading = bullish risk-on. IWM lagging = defensive or large-cap flight to safety.

markets.name: CBOE Volatility Index
markets.whyItMatters: The fear gauge. Measures implied volatility of S&P 500 options. When VIX is elevated, markets are pricing fear. When low, complacency. Both extremes matter.
markets.signalWatched: Above 20 = elevated. Above 25 = reduce risk. Above 35 = fear spike, potential capitulation. Below 15 = complacency, watch for reversal.
markets.cronRule: Check at every 5-min interval. VIX >25: reduce risk, no new adds. VIX >35: defensive posture only.

markets.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Tracks U.S. energy stocks (XOM, CVX, EOG, etc.). Energy sector performance reflects oil/gas demand, geopolitical risk, and inflation regime.
markets.signalWatched: XLE trend vs crude oil. Divergence matters. Rising XLE during equity weakness = inflation regime or geopolitical premium. iNet sells to oil & gas midstream — relevant for Nick's customers.

markets.name: Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Defensive sector. When money flows into XLU, investors are de-risking. Also benefits from AI power demand narrative (data centers need electricity).
markets.signalWatched: XLU relative strength during selloffs = defensive rotation signal. XLU rally in bull market = AI power demand or rate-cut play.

markets.name: Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: The biggest sector in S&P 500. MSFT, AAPL, NVDA dominate. XLK direction = tech sentiment = growth stock environment.
markets.signalWatched: Above 50-day MA = healthy tech. XLK weakness = headwind for every growth name on the board. Track vs QQQ to see mega-cap vs broader tech.

markets.name: Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Banks, insurance, brokerages. XLF strength typically follows yield curve steepening and economic confidence. XLF weakness during stress = credit concern signal.
markets.signalWatched: XLF vs 10Y yield. Banks benefit from steepening curve. Sudden XLF drop = potential credit or banking stress (red flag for whole market).

markets.name: Health Care Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Defensive sector with growth pockets (biotech, MedTech). Relevant for TEM and biotech holdings. Outperforms in risk-off regimes.
markets.signalWatched: XLV leading = defensive rotation. Biotech sub-sector within XLV: watch for breakout patterns that can signal individual stock entry (TEM).

markets.name: Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Defense, aerospace, transportation, machinery. Tracks economic cycle. Strong XLI = expansion confidence. Defense-heavy, which matters with Iran/geopolitical tail.
markets.signalWatched: XLI trend for economic cycle read. Strength during geopolitical risk = defense spending expectations (tailwind for AXON, PLTR, CRWD).

markets.name: Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Chemicals, metals, mining. Inflation-sensitive and global demand indicator. LatAm commodities exposure is relevant for MELI/NU thesis.
markets.signalWatched: XLB strength = commodity/inflation cycle underway. Watch vs DXY — strong dollar hurts materials.

markets.name: Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Rate-sensitive sector. XLRE inversely correlated with 10Y Treasury yield. Useful as a rate-expectations gauge.
markets.signalWatched: XLRE rallying when yields fall = rate-cut trade live. XLRE selling = rates staying higher for longer (bad for growth multiples).

markets.name: Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Amazon, Tesla, Home Depot. Consumer confidence and spending health barometer. When XLY leads, consumer is strong and risk appetite is healthy.
markets.signalWatched: XLY vs XLP spread (discretionary vs staples) = consumer risk appetite. XLY > XLP = healthy consumer, bullish backdrop.

markets.name: Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Defensive haven. Procter & Gamble, Costco, Coca-Cola. When staples outperform, the market is rotating defensive — risk-off signal.
markets.signalWatched: XLP outperforming XLY = defensive rotation underway. Increase caution on growth names.

markets.name: Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Meta, Alphabet, Netflix, Disney. Blend of growth (Meta/Google ad tech) and entertainment. APP competes directly in the ad-tech space.
markets.signalWatched: XLC direction reflects ad-tech and digital media health. Directly relevant to APP thesis — XLC weakness is a headwind for AppLovin.

markets.name: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Proxy for long-duration Treasury bonds and 10Y+ interest rate expectations. When TLT falls, long rates rise — headwind for high-multiple growth stocks. When TLT rallies, rate relief supports growth.
markets.signalWatched: TLT trending up = rates falling = growth stocks get multiple expansion support. TLT breakdown = rising rates = headwind for PLTR, AXON, APP valuations.
markets.cronRule: TLT -1%+ on the day = rate spike, be cautious on high-multiple names.

markets.name: iShares iBoxx High Yield Corporate Bond ETF
markets.whyItMatters: High-yield (junk) corporate bonds. HYG is a real-time credit stress indicator. When HYG drops while equities hold, it's a warning sign — credit markets are pricing pain before stocks do.
markets.signalWatched: HYG diverging down from SPY = credit stress signal, often precedes equity selling. HYG holding up = credit is fine, market backdrop healthy.
markets.cronRule: HYG -0.5%+ while SPY flat/up = credit stress divergence alert. Reduce speculative exposure.

markets.name: U.S. Dollar Index
markets.whyItMatters: Dollar strength/weakness affects everything. Strong DXY: headwind for commodities, EM stocks (MELI, NU), and multinationals. Weak DXY: tailwind for commodities, LatAm, and international growth.
markets.signalWatched: DXY above 106 = EM headwind, reduce MELI/NU. DXY below 100 = EM tailwind, add LatAm. Direction matters more than level.
markets.cronRule: DXY >106: flag EM risk on MELI, NU. DXY <100: flag LatAm opportunity.

markets.name: SPDR Gold Shares ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Gold is the ultimate fear/inflation hedge. GLD rallying = macro uncertainty, inflation fears, or dollar weakness. GLD + VIX both rising = maximum risk-off.
markets.signalWatched: GLD trend direction. Strong GLD + rising VIX = defensive mode. GLD breakout to new highs = inflation or geopolitical stress trade live.

markets.name: iShares Silver Trust ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Silver has both safe-haven and industrial demand (solar, EVs, electronics). Amplifies gold moves. Useful for confirming metals trend.
markets.signalWatched: GLD/SLV ratio: when silver outperforms gold, it signals industrial demand and risk-on within metals. Confirms or denies gold signal.

markets.name: United States Oil Fund ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Crude oil proxy. Oil drives inflation expectations, energy sector performance, and midstream business conditions — all relevant to iNet's customer base.
markets.signalWatched: Oil above $90 = energy stocks outperform, inflation expectations rise, Fed stays hawkish. Oil below $65 = deflationary signal, energy sector headwinds. Midstream pipeline business: oil volume matters even when price is volatile.

markets.name: United States Copper Index Fund
markets.whyItMatters: Copper is the economic doctor. 'Dr. Copper' leads global growth expectations. Rising copper = strong global demand, especially China. Relevant for LatAm exposure (MELI's Brazil) and materials cycle.
markets.signalWatched: Copper rising = global growth confidence. Copper falling = demand contraction fears. Watch for confirming or diverging signal with XLB and IWM.

markets.name: United States Natural Gas Fund ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Natural gas prices affect midstream pipeline and compression companies — directly relevant to iNet's O&G customers. High NG = more pipeline throughput demand.
markets.signalWatched: UNG spike = midstream customers may accelerate capex. UNG collapse = midstream demand destruction risk.

markets.name: ProShares Short Bitcoin ETF (BTC proxy via inverse)
markets.whyItMatters: Bitcoin and crypto assets are a real-time risk appetite gauge. When BTC is up big, risk appetite is high globally. When BTC collapses, speculative risk unwind can bleed into growth equities.
markets.signalWatched: BTC >$100K = extreme risk-on, growth environment. BTC breakdown -20%+ = speculative unwind, potential growth stock headwind. Track via IBIT (Bitcoin ETF) or BTC price.

markets.name: iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Liquid Bitcoin ETF — tracks crypto risk appetite. Crypto performance leads or confirms speculative growth sentiment. NU (Nubank) and MELI both have crypto exposure in their platforms.
markets.signalWatched: IBIT trend: up = risk-on, confirms growth environment. Down 15%+ from recent high = risk-off signal for speculative names.

markets.name: iShares Semiconductor ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Semiconductors lead the tech cycle. NVDA, AMD, AVGO, QCOM. When SOXX is strong, AI infrastructure build-out is confirmed. Weakness here often precedes broader tech weakness.
markets.signalWatched: SOXX above 200-day MA = AI infrastructure cycle intact. SOXX breakdown = AI capex story under question. Directly relevant to all AI-theme names: APP, PLTR.

markets.name: iShares Biotechnology ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Biotech sector health. Relevant for TEM thesis. IBB breakouts often lead individual biotech/health-AI names.
markets.signalWatched: IBB trending up = risk appetite in biotech, TEM setup improves. IBB in downtrend = stay cautious on health-AI names.

markets.name: ARK Innovation ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Cathie Wood's ARKK is a barometer for high-multiple, disruptive growth sentiment. When ARKK is leading, speculative growth is in favor. When ARKK is breaking down, it signals de-rating risk for all high-multiple names on the board.
markets.signalWatched: ARKK trending up = growth multiple expansion environment (good for PLTR, AXON, APP). ARKK weak = compression risk for all high-multiple names.

markets.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield (^TNX)
markets.whyItMatters: The most important number in finance. The 10Y yield is the discount rate for all future cash flows. Rising 10Y = growth stocks reprice lower. Falling 10Y = multiple expansion tailwind.
markets.signalWatched: 10Y above 4.5%: meaningful headwind for high-multiple growth. 10Y falling below 4%: significant tailwind for PLTR, AXON, APP, MELI. Watch daily direction as much as level.
markets.cronRule: 10Y rising >10bps in a single session: reduce high-multiple exposure. 10Y falling sharply: consider adding growth.

markets.name: iShares 7-10 Year Treasury Bond ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Intermediate duration Treasuries. Complement to TLT — less volatile, better signal for medium-term rate expectations. Useful when TLT is distorted by 30Y dynamics.
markets.signalWatched: IEF trend direction as secondary confirmation of TLT signal. IEF + TLT both falling = broad rate rise, caution on growth.

markets.name: iShares MSCI Emerging Markets ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Emerging markets benchmark. Includes Brazil, Mexico, China, India. When EEM is strong, EM currencies are healthy and global risk appetite is up — tailwind for MELI, NU.
markets.signalWatched: EEM strength = EM tailwind for MELI, NU. EEM weakness + strong DXY = dollar squeeze, reduce EM exposure.

markets.name: iShares MSCI Brazil ETF
markets.whyItMatters: Brazil-specific ETF. MELI and NU both have massive Brazil exposure. EWZ performance directly reflects Brazilian market conditions — currency, commodities, politics.
markets.signalWatched: EWZ up = BRL strong, Brazil market healthy = MELI/NU tailwind. EWZ breaking down = increase caution on all LatAm exposure.

cards.starterBuy: Only on pullbacks or after clean execution confirmation; starter size only.
cards.addZone: Add only if deployment + carrier progress continue to improve.
cards.trimZone: Trim into sharp momentum spikes if price outruns milestone progress.
cards.thesisBreak: Meaningful execution delays, weak carrier validation, or financing pressure that changes the risk/reward.
cards.upside: Could become a category-defining communications platform if deployment scales.
cards.downside: High drawdown risk if capital needs or technical delays rise.
cards.summary: Massive TAM with asymmetric upside if direct-to-device works at commercial scale.
cards.commentary: Carrier partnerships and satellite deployment milestones are the key catalysts to watch.

cards.starterBuy: Starter acceptable when trend and execution remain intact.
cards.addZone: Add on proof from Neutron progress and continued backlog quality.
cards.trimZone: Trim partials into euphoric moves disconnected from program milestones.
cards.thesisBreak: Major execution setbacks, repeated delays, or deterioration in space-systems demand.
cards.upside: Can rerate from launch company to broader space infrastructure platform.
cards.downside: Setbacks can create deep multiple compression.
cards.summary: Cleaner emerging space name with several ways to win.
cards.commentary: Neutron progress and launch cadence are the dials to watch.

cards.starterBuy: Wait for more public-company proof and better-understood setup.
cards.addZone: Add only after stronger operating leverage or cleaner adoption proof.
cards.trimZone: Trim if sentiment rerates far ahead of execution.
cards.thesisBreak: Weak adoption proof or inability to show durable economics.
cards.upside: Could become a core intelligence layer in precision medicine.
cards.downside: Young public name with credibility and execution risk.
cards.summary: Combines AI narrative with regulated healthcare workflows.
cards.commentary: Watching for clinical adoption proof and earnings quality improvement.

cards.starterBuy: Starter acceptable here at $450-470 range — 37% off peak with multiple analyst upgrades. Risk/reward is favorable post-correction.
cards.addZone: Add on continued commercial momentum from AXON AI expansion and proof that e-commerce vertical is taking hold.
cards.trimZone: Trim into momentum spikes above $650+, especially if valuation disconnects from near-term growth.
cards.thesisBreak: Material loss of market share in mobile ads, Meta/Google competitive wins, or AXON AI accuracy concerns.
cards.upside: If e-commerce vertical inflects as expected, can rerate significantly higher. Analysts see $700+ base case.
cards.downside: Sentiment-driven stock — can move 30%+ in either direction quickly. Short-seller risk.
cards.summary: AI-driven mobile ad platform with 70% revenue growth FY2025 and 100%+ EPS expansion. AXON AI engine creates durable moat. E-commerce is the next frontier.
cards.commentary: Added 2026-03-23. Post-correction setup — stock off 37% from peak at $734. Strong Buy consensus with 52% upside to avg target. Q4 2025: revenue $1.66B (+21% QoQ), adj EPS $3.24. FY2025 revenue $5.48B (+70%). Multiple upgrades: Needham, Jefferies, Citigroup, UBS all reiterated Strong Buy at dip. Forward PE of ~29x on $15+ EPS estimate is attractive for this growth rate.

cards.starterBuy: Starter acceptable at $135–155. Stock correcting from $207 high on broader tech sell-off — no company-specific issue. Current ~$152 is in the zone.
cards.addZone: Add at $165–180 on Q1 2026 earnings beat and continued AIP commercial acceleration (>100% YoY).
cards.trimZone: Trim partials at $200–215+ approaching 52-week high at $207. Take profits into parabolic strength.
cards.thesisBreak: Below $120 sustained, AIP commercial growth stalls below 80% YoY, or major government contract loss.
cards.upside: Wedbush $230, Truist $223, UBS $200 — bull case $230–260 (+50–70%) on 61% revenue guide and AIP inflection.
cards.downside: Premium valuation at >80x forward earnings — any miss creates outsized downside. High beta stock that can drop 25%+ on macro fear.
cards.summary: AI platform crushing growth: Q4 2025 revenue +70% YoY, U.S. commercial +137% YoY, 2026 guide +61%. Rule of 40 hit 127%. AIP enterprise inflection is real and accelerating.
cards.commentary: Updated 2026-03-24. Old $82–95 zones completely stale — never revisited. PLTR rallied $66 → $207 through 2025, now correcting to ~$152 on broad tech sell-off (no company-specific issue). Q4 2025: Revenue $1.407B (+70% YoY), U.S. Commercial $507M (+137% YoY), record TCV $4.262B (+138%), 954 customers (+34%). Rule of 40 = 127% — elite tier globally. 2026 guidance: $7.19B (+61%) obliterated $6.3B consensus; U.S. commercial >$3.14B (+115%). Analysts: 28 analysts, avg target $196, Wedbush $230 (outperform), UBS $200 (raised Mar 18), Truist $223. Support: $147–150 (recent), $128 (Feb low). Resistance: $163–167, then $207 (52-week high). Starter zone is $135–155 — current price qualifies. Next catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings May 4.

cards.starterBuy: Starter appropriate on any 5-10% pullback. $1,600-1,650 zone is attractive. JP Morgan downgrade to Hold is a potential buying opportunity.
cards.addZone: Add on continued LatAm market expansion, Mercado Pago credit growth, and USD strength reversal.
cards.trimZone: Trim above $2,400-2,500 if LatAm macro deteriorates or currency headwinds intensify.
cards.thesisBreak: Sustained LatAm recession, major Brazilian regulatory action, or Amazon LatAm competitive displacement.
cards.upside: Analysts see $2,733 avg target (+63%). If LatAm fintech matures like U.S., massive re-rate potential.
cards.downside: EM currency risk (BRL, MXN). Macro sensitivity. Recent JP Morgan downgrade signals near-term risk.
cards.summary: The Amazon + PayPal of Latin America. $28.9B revenue in 2025 (+39%). 131M customers on Mercado Pago. Compound growth story with 35%+ expected 2026 growth.
cards.commentary: Added 2026-03-23. Latin American stocks leading in 2026 due to commodity surge and shifting trade alliances. MELI: 16 analysts at Strong Buy, avg target $2,733. JP Morgan downgrade to Hold ($2,100) on March 12 is a potential entry catalyst — the divergence from Street consensus creates opportunity. FY2025 earnings growth slowed (4.5%) due to credit provisioning, but revenue up 39%. Nubank stadium deal signals brand ascent. Strong Buy with patience.

cards.starterBuy: Starter acceptable at $360-395. Reset 4/14/26 after price broke well below the old $490-520 zone. Let the stock prove support before adding size.
cards.addZone: Add on continued ARR growth, Draft One (AI report writing) adoption, and international government wins.
cards.trimZone: Trim above $750 if price gets ahead of FY2027 earnings run-rate.
cards.thesisBreak: Police budget cuts, major TASER recall, AI writing product failure, or loss of government contract momentum.
cards.upside: Strong Buy with $767 avg target (+50%). Baron Opportunity Fund initiated position. $1,000 target from most bullish analyst.
cards.downside: Pulled back hard in early 2026 (-29%). Tariff concerns on hardware. Stock can reprice on macro.
cards.summary: Dominant public safety platform: TASER + body cameras + cloud evidence + AI Draft One. 33% revenue growth FY2025. Recurring revenue model with strong net retention. Q4 beat drove 16% after-hours pop.
cards.commentary: Added 2026-03-23. Q4 2025 earnings beat ended the panic. Revenue $2.78B (+33% FY2025). Strong Buy consensus from 13 of 14 analysts. Iran/geopolitical tensions are a tailwind — domestic security spending increases. Baron Opportunity Fund initiated Q4 2025. Draft One (AI report writing) is the sleeper product — reduces paperwork 80%, officers love it. This is a wide-moat compounder at a 17% discount to recent highs. UBS is the lone Hold.

cards.starterBuy: Starter appropriate at $400-420. Morgan Stanley upgraded March 10. ARR reacceleration confirms post-incident recovery is real.
cards.addZone: Add when Falcon Flex ARR crosses $2B and net new ARR sustains 40%+ growth.
cards.trimZone: Trim above $550-575 if valuation disconnects from ARR trajectory.
cards.thesisBreak: Another major outage, sustained customer churn from July 2024 incident, or consolidation loss to Microsoft/Palo Alto.
cards.upside: 44 analysts, $507 avg target (+22%). Iran war drives urgency for enterprise cybersecurity. ARR $5.25B growing 24%.
cards.downside: July 2024 incident still creates customer trust headwinds. High valuation. Competition from MSFT intensifying.
cards.summary: Best-in-class AI cybersecurity with $5.25B ARR growing 24% YoY. Net new ARR accelerated 47% in Q4. Falcon Flex at $1.69B ARR (+120% YoY). Morgan Stanley named top pick. Iran war = direct tailwind.
cards.commentary: Added 2026-03-23. Morgan Stanley named CRWD top cybersecurity pick with upgrade March 10. RBC reiterated Buy/$550 on March 17. Q4-FY26 strong: ARR $5.25B, net new ARR +47% QoQ. Falcon Flex adoption proves platform stickiness post-incident. Iran conflict driving enterprise urgency to upgrade cyber defenses — CRWD is the premium choice. Citigroup at Strong Buy/$525. The incident is priced in; the recovery is not yet fully priced in.

cards.starterBuy: Pipeline — wait for clearer setup. UBS upgrade to Strong Buy on March 19 is promising. Entry at $14-15 if thesis firms.
cards.addZone: Add on continued credit portfolio growth, Mexico/Colombia expansion proof, and path to U.S. market entry.
cards.trimZone: Trim on sharp runs above $20 or if credit quality deteriorates.
cards.thesisBreak: Material credit loss surge, Brazilian regulatory crackdown, or inability to maintain margin on credit expansion.
cards.upside: Susquehanna sees $22 (+48%). 131M customers — biggest digital bank in the world. Mexico expansion early innings. U.S. market entry potential.
cards.downside: BRL currency exposure. Credit expansion = credit risk. Low analyst coverage (9 analysts only).
cards.summary: World's largest digital bank by customers (131M). Q4 2025 profits up 50%, customer growth 15% YoY. UBS just upgraded to Strong Buy. Inter Miami stadium naming rights signals U.S. brand push.
cards.commentary: Added 2026-03-23. UBS upgraded from Hold to Strong Buy on March 19, raising target to $18. Nubank scores Inter Miami stadium naming rights — clear signal of U.S. market ambition. Q4 2025: gross profit $1.96B, profits +50% YoY. Credit portfolio is the growth engine — expansion is working. LatAm stocks leading 2026 as trade alliances shift. Low analyst coverage (9) means this is under-followed relative to fundamentals. Pipeline for now — watching for U.S. launch catalyst.
